description_long: This is a potted flowering plant with several long, slender green stems emerging from a small brown pot. Each stem supports a single white spathe that curves gently outward, enclosing a yellow spadix, resembling a peace lily (Spathiphyllum). The plant features broad, dark green leaves with prominent veins, arranged in a layered, somewhat overlapping fashion around the base and midsection. The leaves are ovate and have a glossy appearance, with some leaves partially obscured by others. The white spathes are distributed at varying heights, giving the plant a dynamic, natural look. The overall structure is upright and elegant, making it suitable for decorative use in urban interiors or outdoor settings.
description: A potted plant with long green stems, white spathes, yellow spadices, and broad, glossy green leaves arranged in layers. The plant has an upright, elegant appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the layered arrangement of broad green leaves and three prominent white spathes with yellow spadices.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the height of the stems and the vertical distribution of the white spathes above the leaves.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the leaves and the arrangement of the spathes, with more leaves visible and less focus on the spadices.
description_view_3: Side view from the opposite angle, showing the curvature of the stems and the overlapping leaves, with one spadix clearly visible.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals (spathes) are arranged in a whorled configuration, each encircling a central spadix, with some overlapping and slight bilateral symmetry.
